summary: High‑energy second set with soaring Truckin' → Smokestack and fiery Foolish Heart
blurb: The show bursts with a tight second‑set, highlighted by the soaring Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning jam and a double‑Jerry highlight on Foolish > Crazy. Jerry’s guitar is on fire through Foolish Heart and Touch of Grey, while the rhythm section stays rock solid. Several soundboard recordings capture the performance crisply.
review: On April 3, 1991 at The Omni in Atlanta, the Dead delivered a spring‑time blaze that still feels fresh. The first set opened with the upbeat “Touch of Grey” and rolled through classics like “Greatest Story Ever Told,” “Loser,” and a soulful “Brown Eyed Women.” A standout moment arrived early when “The Music Never Stopped” segued into a bright “Don’t Ease Me In,” setting up an energetic flow.  The second set was the night’s high‑point: a tight “Victim Or The Crime” launch led straight into a searing “Foolish Heart,” then into the blistering “Crazy Fingers.”  From there the band launched into an unforgettable “Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning” jam, with Jerry Garcia delivering multiple sparkly solos and a playful lead that earned reviewers the nickname “double‑Jerry gem” when he linked Foolish to Crazy.  The rhythm section—Phil’s solid bass, Mickey’s inventive drumming, and Bill’s steady drive—kept everything grounded while Hornsby and Vince added generous keyboard textures.  Across the available recordings the soundboard sources are generally crisp, capturing the band’s dynamic interplay despite occasional static glitches; a standout matrix audience tape even showcases crystal‑clear low end that brings out the groove of “Touch of Grey” and “Loser.”  Overall, this Omni performance ranks among the tighter spring ’91 shows, offering both tight musicianship and the improvisational spark that defines a great Dead night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the show’s energy, standout jams (Truckin'→Smokestack, Foolish Heart) and Jerry’s fiery playing, while only one negative comment concerns recording cloudiness rather than performance. The balance of positive remarks yields a modest but clear positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with generally crisp audio, captures the key highlights (Truckin'→Smokestack, Foolish>Crazy) and retains vocal/instrument clarity despite minor static.

key_highlights: High‑energy second set featuring a soaring Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning jam
Double‑Jerry highlight on Foolish > Crazy with fiery guitar work
Touch of Grey, Greatest Story Ever Told and Loser receive strong renditions in the matrix recording
Crystal‑clear audience bass on the matrix tape adds extra punch to the groove

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and vocals were immaculate; his solos on Touch, Foolish Heart, Loser, Crazy Fingers and the Truckin' jam were sparkly and relentless, delivering a double‑Jerry highlight on Foolish > Crazy.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a solid, driving bass foundation that locked in tightly with the drums throughout both sets.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick supplied generous keyboard textures; Vince earned specific kudos for his playing, while Hornsby's contributions added depth to the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming was playful—especially during the Truckin' jam—and created space while maintaining a thunderous feel.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann stayed tight and in‑tune, driving the groove with steady power.
